title: Security Management Process
text: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations, including risk analysis, risk management, sanction policy, and information system activity review.

title: Risk Analysis
text: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of electronic protected health information (ePHI).

title: Risk Management
text: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level.

title: Assigned Security Responsibility
text: Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures required by the Security Rule.

title: Workforce Security
text: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of the workforce have appropriate access to ePHI and to prevent those who do not have access from obtaining it.

title: Information Access Management
text: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to ePHI consistent with the applicable requirements of the Privacy Rule.

title: Security Awareness and Training
text: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of the workforce, including periodic security reminders, malware protection, log-in monitoring, and password management.

title: Security Incident Procedures
text: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents, including identifying and responding to suspected or known incidents and documenting outcomes.

title: Contingency Plan
text: Establish and implement policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence that damages systems containing ePHI, including data backup, disaster recovery, and emergency mode operation plans.

title: Evaluation
text: Perform periodic technical and nontechnical evaluations establishing the extent to which security policies and procedures meet the requirements of the Security Rule.

title: Business Associate Contracts
text: Obtain satisfactory assurances, documented through a written contract, that a business associate will appropriately safeguard ePHI.

title: Facility Access Controls
text: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to electronic information systems and the facilities in which they are housed, while ensuring that properly authorized access is allowed.

title: Workstation Use
text: Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed and the manner in which they are to be performed on workstations that access ePHI.

title: Workstation Security
text: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access ePHI to restrict access to authorized users.

title: Device and Media Controls
text: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain ePHI, including disposal and media re-use.

title: Access Control
text: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain ePHI to allow access only to authorized persons or software programs, including unique user identification and emergency access.

title: Encryption and Decryption
text: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI at rest.

title: Audit Controls
text: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI.

title: Integrity
text: Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction.

title: Person or Entity Authentication
text: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed, including multi-factor authentication where appropriate.

title: Transmission Security
text: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to ePHI being transmitted over an electronic communications network, including encryption in transit.

title: Business Associate Arrangements
text: Document the satisfactory assurances required by the business associate contract requirements through a written agreement that meets applicable requirements.

title: Policies and Procedures
text: Implement reasonable and appropriate policies and procedures to comply with the standards and implementation specifications of the Security Rule.

title: Documentation
text: Maintain the policies and procedures implemented to comply with the Security Rule in written form, and retain documentation for six years from the date of its creation or last effective date.
